zh.lifeSummary: 當Lady Elizabeth Bourchier於1475年出生在英國英格蘭戴文可姆 在 泰尼黑阿德時，她的父親Fulke Bourchier 10th Baron FitzWarin30歲，母親Elizabeth Dinham26歲。她與Sir Edward Stanhope在英國坎布利其爾藍普頓結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。她去世於1557年8月8日在英國英格蘭米德萊可可拉肯維爾享年82歲，埋葬在英國米德萊可可拉肯維爾 聖 詹姆斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Lady Elizabeth Bourchier于1475年出生在英国英格兰戴文可姆 在 泰尼黑阿德时，她的父亲Fulke Bourchier 10th Baron FitzWarin30岁，母亲Elizabeth Dinham26岁。她与Sir Edward Stanhope在英国坎布利其爾藍普頓结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。她去世于1557年8月8日在英国英格兰米德萊可可拉肯維爾享年82岁，埋葬在英国米德萊可可拉肯維爾 聖 詹姆斯。

nameMeanings.name: Bourchier
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: 1  English: perhaps a variant of   Burcher   (  see  Bircher  ). Some of the following early bearers may alternatively belong under (2). The name has sometimes been altered to or confused with   Bouchier   and   Boucher  .

2 Norman, English: locative name of uncertain origin but it is presumably from a French place-name, perhaps Boursières in Haute-Saône. The name was early confused with other surnames and may survive in Bouchier, Bowcher, Bowser, and Boozer . The surname gave rise to a number of minor place-names in Essex: Bourchier’s alias Bouchier’s Hall in Aldham, Bourchier’s Hall in Messing; Bouchier’s Grange in Great Coggeshall, Bouchiers Chapel in Tollesbury, Boarstye Fm and Bowser’s Hall in Rivenhall (Essex), and Boose’s Green in Colne Engayne.

variants.name: Bourcier
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Laurent, Lucien, Armand, Emile.
French: variant of   Boursier   and, in North America, (also) an altered form of this.
History: The (majority of the) American bearers of the surname Bourcier are descendants of Jean Boursier dit Lavigne from France (see Boursier ).

variants.name: Bircher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Erwin, Albrecht, Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ulrich.
South German and Swiss German: topographic name for someone who lived by a birch tree or in a birch wood, from Middle High German birche ‘birch’ + the suffix -er denoting an inhabitant.
English: habitational name from Birchover in Derbyshire or Bircher in Hereford, both named as from Old English birce ‘birch’ + ofer ‘ridge’.

variants.name: Bouchier
meanings: 1  English: variant of   Butcher  .
2  English: alternatively a variant of   Belcher  .
3  Norman, English: possibly a variant of   Bourchier   (2).

variants.name: Bowser
meanings: English: nickname from either from Old French beu cher ‘good company’, or from Old French beu sire ‘fair sir’ (see Belcher ), given either to a fine gentleman (perhaps ironically), or to someone who made frequent use of this term of address. Compare Bonser and Boozer .
English: variant of   Bolsover  .
Americanized form of German   Bauser  .

variants.name: Boozer
meanings: Americanized form of Swiss German   Buser  .
English (Kent): variant of   Bowser   1.

variants.name: Burcher
meanings: English: variant of   Bircher  .

variants.name: Boucher
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Normand, Andre, Marcel, Emile, Gilles, Lucien, Yvon, Adrien, Jacques, Laurent, Alcide.

French: occupational name for a butcher or slaughterer, from Old French bouchier, a derivative of bouc ‘ram’. Compare Bouche 2, Bouchey , Bouchie , Boushey , Boushie , Bush 5, Busha 1, Busher , and Bushey 3.
English: variant of   Butcher  , a cognate of 1 above.

variants.name: Borger
meanings: North German (also Börger) and Dutch: variant of Burger .
Jewish (Ashkenazic): nickname from Yiddish borger ‘borrower’ or ‘lender’.
Norwegian and Swedish: habitational name for someone from a place called Borg (see   Borg  ), or a topographic name for someone living by a fortification or near a hill resembling a fortification.

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Lady Elizabeth Bourchier was born in 1475, in Combe in Teignhead, Devon, England, United Kingdom, her father, Fulke Bourchier 10th Baron FitzWarin, was 30 and her mother, Elizabeth Dinham, was 26. She married Sir Edward Stanhope in Rampton, Cambridgeshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. She died on 8 August 1557, in Clerkenwell, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 82, and was buried in Clerkenwell St James, Middlesex, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Lady Elizabeth Bourchier est née en 1475, à Combe in Teignhead, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Fulke Bourchier 10th Baron FitzWarin, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Dinham, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Sir Edward Stanhope à Rampton, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Elle est décédée le 8 août 1557, à Clerkenwell, Middlesex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 82 ans, et a été enterrée à Clerkenwell St James, Middlesex, Angleterre.

Sie hat in Rampton, Cambridgeshire, England Sir Edward Stanhope geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Sie starb am 8. August 1557 im Alter von 82 Jahren in Clerkenwell, Middlesex, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Clerkenwell St James, Middlesex, England begraben.

ko.Clifford in Dunsford, Combeinteignhead,: 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 코브 인 테익헤드
ko.lifeSummary: Lady Elizabeth Bourchier(이)가 1475년 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 코브 인 테익헤드에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Fulke Bourchier 10th Baron FitzWarin(은)는 30세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Dinham(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 영국 캄브리지실르 램프턴에서 Sir Edward Stanhope(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1557년 8월 8일 영국 잉글랜드 미들레세스 클레르켄웰에서 82세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 미들레세스 클레르켄웰 스트 자메스에 묻혔다.

ru.Clifford in Dunsford, Combeinteignhead,: комб ин тиинид, девон, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Lady Elizabeth Bourchier родилась в 1475 г. в местности комб ин тиинид, девон, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени Fulke Bourchier 10th Baron FitzWarin было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Dinham, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir Edward Stanhope в местности рамптон, камбриджешир, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Она умерла 8 августа 1557 г. в местности клиркенвелл, миддлесекс, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 82 лет и была похоронена в местности клиркенвелл ст джемесь, миддлесекс, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Lady Elizabeth Bourchier nació en 1475, en Combe in Teignhead, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Fulke Bourchier 10th Baron FitzWarin, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Dinham, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Sir Edward Stanhope en Rampton, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió el 8 de agosto de 1557, en Clerkenwell, Middlesex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 82 años, y fue sepultada en Clerkenwell St James, Middlesex, Inglaterra.
